problem: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism?
mechanism: Deep brain stimulation of subthalamic nucleus
hypothesis: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Deep. Deep brain stimulation of subthalamic nucleus directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: fMRI connectivity study with 4 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: What we observe is not nature itself but nature exposed to our method of questioning. — Heisenberg
